risks: No monitoring or diagnostics resource (e.g., Log Analytics workspace) is included — limits observability across the pipeline
Public network access is enabled on ADF, Synapse, and Databricks — increases attack surface; should be restricted for production
Storage account local auth (key access) is not explicitly disabled — WAF security best practice recommends disabling key-based access when using RBAC
Standard_LRS storage provides no zone redundancy — risk of data loss during a zone outage; plan acknowledges this as a dev trade-off
Databricks workspace does not declare Key Vault as a dependency despite the plan noting Databricks tokens are stored in Key Vault
correctionsRecommended: Add a Log Analytics workspace resource and configure diagnostic settings on each resource for centralized monitoring and alerting
Add Key Vault (kv-salespipeline-dev-001) to the Databricks workspace dependencies array to reflect the runtime secret dependency
Explicitly set allowSharedKeyAccess to false on the storage account to disable local auth per WAF Security pillar guidance
Consider adding a managed VNet configuration and private endpoints on ADF, Synapse, and storage for network isolation before production
Add resource tagging strategy (e.g., environment, project, cost-center) for operational excellence and cost tracking
Plan for storage redundancy upgrade from LRS to ZRS or GRS before moving to production workloads
